title: Sappho fragments
why_it_fits: Sappho remains one of the ancestral lyric sources for queer poetic memory. Her fragments survive as broken transmission, desire, address, and absence.
rights_note: Public-domain translations are available through Project Gutenberg, but any modern translation must be checked separately.

title: Goblin Market and Other Poems
why_it_fits: Rossetti's work carries charged forms of sisterhood, desire, religious discipline, bodily threat, and survival. It can be read beside queer and feminist archival hunger.
rights_note: Public-domain text available through Project Gutenberg.

title: Leaves of Grass
why_it_fits: Whitman's poetry offers bodily collectivity, erotic comradeship, democratic address, and a restless idea of belonging.
rights_note: Public-domain text available through Project Gutenberg.

title: The Poems of Emily Dickinson
why_it_fits: Dickinson's compressed poems hold enclosure, refusal, death, interior weather, and radical forms of address.
rights_note: Public-domain editions are available through Project Gutenberg. Specific editions and editorial versions should still be checked.

title: Songs to Joannes
why_it_fits: Mina Loy's work fractures gender, sexuality, body, and modernity with sharp avant-garde force.
rights_note: Rights status may vary. Use as metadata only unless a reliable public-domain or licensed source is verified.

title: Poems and fragments
why_it_fits: Renée Vivien's work is central to lesbian poetic lineage, erotic address, melancholy, and mythic self-fashioning.
rights_note: Author died in 1909, so many works are public domain in many jurisdictions. Verify edition and source before reproducing.

title: Poetic works
poet: Sor Juana Inés de la Cruz
why_it_fits: Sor Juana's writing offers feminist intellect, religious constraint, desire, knowledge, and defiance against institutional authority.
rights_note: Original works are public domain. Modern translations require separate rights checks.

title: Poems and songs
why_it_fits: Papusza is central to Romani poetic memory, displacement, song, survival, and the violence of being translated into public visibility.
rights_note: Do not reproduce text without verified permission or public-domain confirmation. Use metadata only.

title: Romani oral song and lament traditions
why_it_fits: Romani oral song traditions carry memory, grief, movement, family, persecution, celebration, and survival outside institutional archives.
rights_note: Treat oral traditions carefully. Do not extract or reproduce without context, consent, and source verification.
